revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24167314344|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:市司]]
{{Otheruses|Ritsuryō-era government agency overseeing markets|Other uses of the term "市司"|qq=}} '''Ichitsukasa''' (市司) was a government agency under the {{ill|Kyōshiki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōshiki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. It managed the markets located in the capital, with the '''Tōichitsukasa''' (東市司) under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Sakyōshiki|ja|左京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyōshiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}} and the '''Saiichitsukasa''' (西市司) under the {{ill|Ukyōshiki|ja|右京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyōshiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}}. == History == During the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato period|1=Yamato Kingship}} (Yamato court), markets such as {{ill|Tsubakiichi|ja|海石榴市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubakiichi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 海柘榴市 (大和国)}} and {{ill|Karunoichi|ja|軽市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karunoichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Asuka, and {{ill|Ekaichi|ja|餌香市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ekaichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Adokuwanoichi|ja|阿斗桑市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adokuwanoichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} were politically utilized at key transportation hubs. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} references administrators called "Ichitsukasa" (市司) starting in the {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|qid=Q1077930|lt=Taika era|1=Taika (era)}}, though it is unclear whether they were addointed officials or market representatives addroved by the court.<ref name="market_management">Miyakawa, "Market Management under the Ritsuryō System"</ref> With the construction of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|qid=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}} and the enactment of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}, markets previously scattered across regions were centralized within the capital, forming the East and West Markets. The establishment of Ichitsukasa as a formal management agency is believed to coincide with this centralization. According to the {{ill|Fusō Ryakuki|en|Fusō Ryakuki|qid=Q11076282|lt=Fusō Ryakki|1=Fusō Ryakuki}} and {{ill|Teiō Henki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiō Henki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550950}}, the East and West Markets were first established in {{ill|Taihō 3|ja|大宝3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135550951|1=703}} (703).<ref name="market_management"/> Additionally, the markets and Ichitsukasa were exclusive to the capital. With each relocation of the capital ({{ill|Sento|ja|遷都|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sento|1=capital city relocation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q558566}}), the markets, Ichitsukasa, and merchants were moved accordingly.<ref name="capital_markets">Miyakawa, "Capital Markets and the East and West Markets"</ref> Unlike in Tang China, where Ichitsukasa existed at both the central and local levels, Japan did not establish government-operated markets or Ichitsukasa in provincial areas. Instead, {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}} relied on preexisting markets created by local aristocrats or officials, including {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}}, at transportation hubs.<ref name="market_management"/> == Functions == Ichitsukasa supervised the East and West Markets in the capital. Its responsibilities included: - Preventing fraud and crime in the markets. - Managing weights and measures ({{ill|Doryōkō|ja|度量衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Doryōkō|1=weights and measures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13862405}}). - Monitoring commodity prices. - Procuring goods for government use when necessary. Ichitsukasa also created price survey reports called {{ill|Kokachō|ja|估価帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokachō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. These reports, compiled every ten days (or monthly as per the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}), classified prices into three tiers: udder, middle, and lower, with further subcategories of high, medium, and low (resulting in nine levels overall). The reports were submitted to the relevant {{ill|Kyōshiki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōshiki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}}, serving as a reference for fair pricing in government procurement.<ref>Mori Akihiko, "Characteristics of Ancient Japan's Value Systems and Currency," in *Studies on Japan's Ancient Currency System* (Hanawa Shobō, 2016), ISBN 978-4-8273-1283-6</ref> The head of Ichitsukasa, the '''Ichinokami''' (市正), was originally a {{ill|Shōroku-i-jō|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Udder Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} Rank official. However, in practice, experienced bureaucrats of the Fifth Rank, {{ill|Tokugyōsei|ja|得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugyōsei|1=Tokugōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97212787}}, or {{ill|Monjōsei|ja|文章生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōsei|1=Monjōshō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97378717}} were often addointed, especially those with financial acumen. {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}} officials sometimes concurrently held the role to oversee market security. Suddorting personnel included: * '''Katana''' (価長), responsible for price inspections. * '''Mononobe''' (物部), tasked with crime control in the markets. == Personnel == * Ichinokami (市正): 1 for East and West Markets each ({{ill|Shōroku-i-jō|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Udder Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} Rank). * Ichinoyu (市佑): 1 for each market ({{ill|Jūshichii-ge|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshichii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}} Rank). * Ichinoreishi (市令史): 1 for each market ({{ill|Dai-shoi-jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai-shoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}} Rank). Additional staff: - Clerks (史生): 2 for each market (later reduced to 1). - Katana (価長): 5 for each market. - Mononobe (物部): 20 for each market. - Assistants (使部): 10 for each market (later reduced to 6). - Stewards (直丁): 1 for each market. == Notes == <references/> == References == * Miyakawa, Maki. *Trade and Society in Ancient Japan*. Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2020. ISBN 978-4-642-04658-9. *** Chapter 1, "Market Management under the Ritsuryō System" (dd. 12–42; original paper, 2011). *** Chapter 2, "Capital Markets and the East and West Markets" (dd. 43–59; original paper, 2012). == See also == * {{ill|Ichichō|ja|市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichichō|1=Ichimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18700606}} * {{ill|Kokahō|ja|估価法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokahō|1=Koka Method|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381504}} * {{ill|Nawa Nagatoshi|en|Nawa Nagatoshi|qid=Q11415325|lt=Nawa Nagatoshi|1=Nawa Nagatoshi}} * {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}} {{translated page|ja|市司|version=100147652|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:市司]]
<!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--><!--City Offices (Tōji and Sai)--> [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== '''Market Bureau (市司)''' (いちのつかさ) was an organization under the {{ill|Kyōshiki|ja|京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōshiki|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}} in the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. It was established to manage the markets in the capital city, with separate bureaus for the east and west markets. The '''East Market Bureau''' (東市司, Higashi no Ichi no Tsukasa) was under the {{ill|Sakyōshiki|ja|左京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyōshiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}}, while the '''West Market Bureau''' (西市司, Nishi no Ichi no Tsukasa) was under the {{ill|Ukyōshiki|ja|右京職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukyōshiki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374719}}.
== History == During the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}} period, markets such as {{ill|Tsubaki Market|ja|海石榴市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubaki Market|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 海柘榴市 (大和国)}} and {{ill|Karuno Market|ja|軽市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karuno Market|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Asuka, as well as {{ill|Ega Market|ja|餌香市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ega Market|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Adogusa Market|ja|阿斗桑市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Adogusa Market|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}}, developed as hubs of trade along key transportation routes. These markets were likely utilized for political purposes. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} mentions "Market Bureau" administrators during the {{ill|Taika era|ja|大化年間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika era|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, but it is unclear if these were royal officials or representatives recognized by the court.<ref name="market_management">Miyagawa, "Market Management under the Ritsuryō System."</ref>
Following the establishment of {{ill|Fujiwara-kyō|en|Fujiwara-kyō|qid=Q1196381|lt=Fujiwara-kyō|1=Fujiwara-kyō}} and the enactment of the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}, markets were consolidated within the capital. The {{ill|Fusō Ryakuki|en|Fusō Ryakuki|qid=Q11076282|lt=Fusō Ryakuki|1=Fusō Ryakuki}} and {{ill|Teiō Hennenki|ja|帝王編年記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teiō Hennenki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135550950}} record the creation of the east and west markets in {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Taihō|1=Taiho}} 3 (703 CE), and the Market Bureau system was likely established at that time.<ref name="market_management"/>
In contrast to Tang China, where Market Bureaus existed both in the capital and in provincial regions, Japan limited such institutions to the capital. Local markets remained managed by local elites, including {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}}, or naturally developed near transportation hubs.<ref name="market_management"/>
== Functions == The Market Bureau managed the east and west markets, overseeing trade regulations, enforcing law and order, and ensuring accurate {{ill|weights and measures|ja|度量衡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/weights and measures|1=weights and measures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13862405}}. The bureau also monitored {{ill|Price index|en|Price index|qid=Q631360|lt=commodity prices|1=Price index}} and created a "Price Register" (估価帳) every ten days (or monthly as specified in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}). Prices were categorized into three grades (high, medium, low), with subcategories forming a nine-grade system. This register helped maintain fair pricing for government procurement.<ref>Mori Akihiko, "The Characteristics of Ancient Japanese Value Systems and Currency," in *Research on the Ancient Japanese Monetary System* (Hanawa Shobō, 2016). ISBN 978-4-8273-1283-6</ref>
The head of the bureau, called the '''Ichinokami''' (市正), was a {{ill|Shōroku-i-jō|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} Rank official. However, fifth-Rank bureaucrats or individuals with administrative skills were often appointed. As the bureau also handled market security, some officers concurrently held positions as {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=}}.
Additional staff included {{ill|Kachō|ja|価長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kachō|qq=}}, who inspected prices, and {{ill|Mononobe officials|ja|物部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe officials|qq=}}, who managed law enforcement.
== Staff == * '''Ichinokami''' (市正): {{ill|Shōroku-i-jō|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku-i-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} Rank, one each for East and West * '''Yū''' (佑): {{ill|Jūshichii-ge|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshichii-ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従七位}} Rank, one each for East and West * '''Reishi''' (令史): {{ill|Daishoi-jō|ja|大初位上|1=Senior Initial Rank|lt=Senior Initial Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishoi-jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大初位}} Rank, one each for East and West * '''Shisei''' (史生): Two (later one) each for East and West * '''Kachō''' (価長): Five each for East and West * '''Mononobe officials''' (物部): Twenty each for East and West * '''Shibu''' (使部): Ten (later six) each for East and West * '''Chokuchō''' (直丁): One each for East and West
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * Miyagawa, Maki. *Ancient Japanese Trade and Society* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 2020). ISBN 978-4-642-04658-9. ** Part I, Chapter 1: "Market Management under the Ritsuryō System" (pp. 12–42; original article, 2011). ** Part I, Chapter 2: "City Systems and East-West Markets" (pp. 43–59; original article, 2012).
== See Also == * {{ill|Ichimachi|ja|市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Koka Method|ja|估価法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koka Method|qq=}} * {{ill|Nawa Nagatoshi|en|Nawa Nagatoshi|qid=Q11415325|lt=Nawa Nagatoshi|1=Nawa Nagatoshi}} * {{ill|Katagiri Katsumoto|en|Katagiri Katsumoto|qid=Q1882775|lt=Katagiri Katsumoto|1=Katagiri Katsumoto}}
{{translated page|ja|市司|version=100147652|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:%E5%B8%82%E5%8F%B8]]
<!--Ichinotsukasa-->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q11480073}} [[Category:Officials of the Ritsuryō System]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
